en: There is an evil that I have seen under the sun, and it lies heavy on mankind:
zh: 我看見日光之下有一件憾事，重壓在世人身上。

en: a man to whom God gives wealth, possessions, and honor, so that he lacks nothing of all that he desires, yet God does not give him power to enjoy them, but a stranger enjoys them. This is vanity; it is a grievous evil.
zh: 蒙 神賜予財富、資產和榮譽的人，心裡所願的，都一無所缺，可是 神使他無力享用，反而有外人來享用。這是虛空，也是禍患。

en: If a man fathers a hundred children and lives many years, so that the days of his years are many, but his soul is not satisfied with life's good things, and he also has no burial, I say that a stillborn child is better off than he.
zh: 人如果生下百子，活了許多歲數，年日長久，但是心裡不因美物滿足，又得不到安葬，我以為流產的胎比他還好。

en: For it comes in vanity and goes in darkness, and in darkness its name is covered.
zh: 因為這胎虛虛而來，暗暗而去，黑暗把他的名字遮蔽了。

en: Moreover, it has not seen the sun or known anything, yet it finds rest rather than he.
zh: 這胎既看不見天日，又一無所知，就比那人更得安息。

en: Even though he should live a thousand years twice over, yet enjoy no good- do not all go to the one place?
zh: 那人即使活了一千年，再活一千年，也享受不到甚麼；他們兩者不是都歸一個地方嗎？

en: All the toil of man is for his mouth, yet his appetite is not satisfied.
zh: 人的一切勞碌都為口腹，他的心卻還是填不滿。

en: For what advantage has the wise man over the fool? And what does the poor man have who knows how to conduct himself before the living?
zh: 智慧人比愚昧人有甚麼長處呢？窮人在眾人面前知道怎樣行事為人，又有甚麼長處呢？

en: Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of the appetite: this also is vanity and a striving after wind.
zh: 眼睛看見的，勝過心中想望的。這也是虛空，也是捕風。

en: Whatever has come to be has already been named, and it is known what man is, and that he is not able to dispute with one stronger than he.
zh: 已經存在的，早已有了名稱；大家都知道人是怎樣的；人絕對無法與比自己強的抗辯。

en: The more words, the more vanity, and what is the advantage to man?
zh: 因為話語增多，虛空也增多，這對人有甚麼益處呢？

en: For who knows what is good for man while he lives the few days of his vain life, which he passes like a shadow? For who can tell man what will be after him under the sun?
zh: 在虛空、消逝如影的人生，短暫的日子裡，有誰知道甚麼是對人有益處呢？誰能告訴人他死後在日光之下會發生甚麼事呢？
